_schema.description: Per-region, per-month rainfall and temperature baselines used by the seasonal-anomaly drought filter. Without this, the drought flag fires whenever absolute soil moisture is low — even in regions naturally dry for the time of year (e.g. Brazilian Cerrado in July). With seasonal baselines, drought is flagged only when current conditions sit > N standard deviations BELOW the historical norm for THAT region × month.

region_key._meta: Free-text label, lat/lng or source attribution

backfill_plan: 1. Pull CHIRPS daily precipitation 1991-2020 for each region polygon
2. Pull ERA5 daily 2m temperature 1991-2020 for the same polygons
3. Aggregate to month-level: mean, std, p10, p90 per (region, calendar_month)
4. Emit JSON with the schema above
5. Wire `seasonal_z_score(region, month, current_value)` into _drought_score in each `*_weather.py`
_schema.status: scaffold — backfill pending. Until populated, the seasonal filter is bypassed and existing absolute-threshold drought logic continues to apply.
